q.en: What is Japan's basic landform, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据本书，日本基本的地形是什么？

options.en: An archipelago, a string of islands stretching off the east coast of Asia
options.zh: 一个群岛，即伸延在亚洲东岸外的一串岛屿

options.en: A single large continent
A landlocked country
A desert peninsula

explain.en: The book says Japan 'is an archipelago, a string of islands that stretches more than 2,400 km ... just off the east coast of Asia in the Pacific Ocean.'
explain.zh: 书中说日本“是一个群岛，一串延伸超过2400公里的岛屿……位于太平洋中亚洲东岸外”。

q.en: Where is Japan's capital, and about how many people live in its metropolitan area?
q.zh: 日本的首都在哪里？其都市区大约有多少人口？

options.en: Tokyo, on the island of Honshu, with more than 32 million people in the metro area
options.zh: 东京，在本州岛上，都市区人口超过3200万

options.en: Kyoto, with about 5 million people
Osaka, with about 10 million people
Hokkaido, with about 1 million people

explain.en: The book states the capital is Tokyo on Honshu, and 'More than thirty-two million people live and work in this sprawling metropolis.'
explain.zh: 书中说首都是位于本州的东京，“超过3200万人在这个 sprawling 大都市生活和工作”。

q.en: What and where is Japan's highest peak?
q.zh: 日本的最高峰是什么？在哪里？

options.en: Mount Fuji, a volcano on the southern coast of Honshu
options.zh: 富士山，位于本州南海岸的一座火山

options.en: Mount Honshu, in the center of Kyushu
Mount Tokyo, in the capital
Mount Hokkaido, in the north

explain.en: The book says: 'The highest peak is Mount Fuji, a volcano on the southern coast of Honshu.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“最高峰是富士山，位于本州南海岸的一座火山。”

q.en: What does the book say about Japan's emperor and the Yamato dynasty?
q.zh: 关于日本的天皇和山户（Yamato）王朝，书中说了什么？

options.en: The emperor has come from the Yamato dynasty for more than two thousand years, the oldest continuous monarchy in the world
options.zh: 天皇出自山户王朝已超过两千年，是世界上延续最久的君主制

options.en: The emperor is elected every four years
The dynasty began only after World War II
options.zh: 该王朝仅在二战后才开始

options.en: Japan has no emperor

explain.en: The book states the emperor 'has come from the same family, the Yamato dynasty, for more than two thousand years. It is the oldest continuous monarchy in the world.'
explain.zh: 书中说天皇“出自同一个家族——山户王朝——已超过两千年。这是世界上延续最久的君主制。”

q.en: At the end, the book gives a 'Quick Facts' box with population, land area, capital, language, religions, and currency. What is the purpose of this box?
q.zh: 在结尾，本书给出“速览事实”框，包含人口、面积、首都、语言、宗教和货币。这个框的作用是什么？

options.en: To summarize the country's key statistics at a glance
options.zh: 为了一目了然地概括该国的主要数据

options.en: To list the photo credits
To define vocabulary words
To translate the book into English
options.zh: 为了把本书翻译成英文

explain.en: The 'Quick Facts' box compiles Population (127,250,000), Land, Capital City (Tokyo), Primary Language, Primary Religions, and Currency (Yen) for quick reference.
explain.zh: “速览事实”框汇总了人口（127,250,000）、面积、首都（东京）、主要语言、主要宗教和货币（日元），方便快速查阅。

options.en: By topics such as location, people, land, history, celebrations, and food
options.zh: 按位置、人民、土地、历史、节庆和饮食等主题组织

options.en: Alphabetically by city name
Strictly by year, from ancient to modern
Only by the size of each island

explain.en: The table of contents lists sections: Where Is It?, People, Land, History, Celebrations, Food, and Conclusion--topical chapters rather than a timeline.
explain.zh: 目录列出的章节有：在哪里、人民、土地、历史、节庆、饮食和结论——是按主题而非时间线组织的。

q.en: The book explains that most of Japan is mountainous. How does this affect where cities are built?
q.zh: 书中说日本多山。这对城市的选址有何影响？

options.en: Because the land is so mountainous, nearly all cities are located in the flat coastal areas
options.zh: 由于山地太多，几乎所有城市都建在平坦的沿海地区

options.en: Cities are built on the highest mountain peaks
options.zh: 城市建在最高的山峰上

options.en: Mountains make people prefer to live on distant islands
options.zh: 山地使人们更愿住在遥远的岛屿

options.en: The mountains have no effect on city location
options.zh: 山地对城市位置没有影响

explain.en: The book says 'most of Japan is mountainous, which is why nearly all of its cities are located in the flat coastal areas.'
explain.zh: 书中说“日本大部多山，这就是为什么几乎所有城市都位于平坦的沿海地区”。

q.en: The book defines a tsunami. What causes one, according to the text?
q.zh: 本书定义了海啸。根据文意，海啸是由什么引起的？

options.en: An earthquake along the ocean floor creates a giant wave called a tsunami, meaning 'harbor wave'
options.zh: 海底发生的地震引发巨大的海浪，称为海啸，意为“港口的波”

options.en: A type of seasonal monsoon wind
A volcanic mountain that erupts
A traditional rice-planting festival

explain.en: The book says an earthquake along the ocean floor 'can create a giant wave called a tsunami (tsoo-NAH-mee), which means 'harbor wave' in Japanese.'
explain.zh: 书中说海底发生的地震“会引发巨大的海浪，称为海啸（tsunami），在日语中意为‘港口的波’”。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book about Japan?
q.zh: 作者写这本关于日本的书的意图是什么？

options.en: To introduce readers to Japan's land, people, culture, and history
options.zh: 为了向读者介绍日本的土地、人民、文化和历史

options.en: To argue that Japan should expand its territory
options.zh: 为了论证日本应扩张领土

options.en: To entertain with a fictional story
options.zh: 为了用虚构故事取悦读者

options.en: To teach readers how to cook sushi

explain.en: As a 'Countries Around the World' book, it surveys Japan's location, people, landforms, history, celebrations, and food to inform readers about the country.
explain.zh: 作为“世界各国”系列图书，它概览日本的位置、人民、地形、历史、节庆和饮食，向读者介绍这个国家。

q.en: The conclusion says 'Timeless traditions and customs mingle with cutting-edge technology.' What point is the author making?
q.zh: 结论说“永恒的传统与习俗和最前沿的科技交融”。作者想表达什么？

options.en: Japan blends ancient traditions with modern technology, embracing both worlds
options.zh: 日本把古老传统与现代科技融合，兼收并蓄两个世界

options.en: Japan rejects all modern technology
options.zh: 日本排斥一切现代科技

options.en: Japan has no real traditions
Japan is only a rural farming society
options.zh: 日本只是个乡村农耕社会

explain.en: The conclusion states Japan 'embraces two worlds--the ancient and the modern,' noting crowded high-tech cities alongside rice farming and old customs.
explain.zh: 结论说日本“兼收两个世界——古代与现代”，既有人满为患的高科技都市，也有种稻和传统习俗。

prompt.en: The book says Japan mixes ancient traditions with modern technology. Which Japanese custom or modern feature mentioned in the book interests you most, and why?
prompt.zh: 书中说日本把古老传统与现代科技融合。书中提到的哪种日本习俗或现代特色最让你感兴趣？为什么？

en: Pick one specific detail from the book, such as tatami mats, bullet trains, the tea ceremony, or Mount Fuji.
Explain why it interests you and connect it to the idea of blending old and new.
zh: 从书中选出一處具体细节，例如榻榻米、新干线、茶道或富士山。
解释它为何吸引你，并将其与“新旧交融”的主题联系起来。
